title: CI: enable root user namespaces
body:
Oops.

Fixes: #23383 

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Also cherrypicked and running in #17831 and #23275
Cockpit tests failed for commit dc33baa0583d459865f640198dd702ab52c31cc4. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Well, look what happens when we don't actually run our tests :-(

> Well, look what happens when we don't actually run our tests :-(

Haha why do you think I didn't open a PR and decided to file an issue. Because knowing how we review tests I have to assume not running => broken (sooner or later)

int tests were easy. [sys](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5898057223503872/html/sys-podman-debian-13-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00109) are beyond my ken:
```
[+0288s] not ok 109 [030] podman run - rootfs with idmapped mounts in 1764ms
...
<+012ms> # # podman run --uidmap=0:1000:10000 --rm --rootfs /tmp/CI_Wxqp/podman_bats.1rNMIZ/rootfs:idmap=uids=0-1001-10000;gids=0-1002-10000 stat -c %u:%g /bin
<+066ms> # Error: failed to create idmapped mount: operation not permitted
```
First problem: failure does not reproduce on my f40 laptop.

~~Second problem: test is actually broken, needs a fix:~~
```diff
-    run_podman run --rm --uidmap=0:1000:10000 -v volume:/volume:idmap --rootfs $romount stat -c %u:%g /volume
+    run_podman run --rm --uidmap=0:1000:10000 -v $myvolume:/volume:idmap --rootfs $romount stat -c %u:%g /volume
```
~~When I fix it, test fails on my laptop with:~~ Never mind, I fixed problem 2. Problem 1 is still a problem.
```
[08:49:52.744732625] # /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman run --rm --uidmap=0:1000:10000 -v my-volume-t1-w7nuobt1:/volume:idmap --rootfs /tmp/podman_bats.kC6FCy/rootfs stat -c %u:%g /volume/myfile
   [08:49:52.858913405] Error: mapping user 0:0: host ID 0 cannot be mapped to a container ID
   [08:49:52.861820205] [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
Any userns experts care to weigh in?
@giuseppe PTAL if you have time

* [debian](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5094598798737408/html/sys-podman-debian-13-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00109): operation not permitted
* [f39](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4531648845316096/html/sys-podman-fedora-39-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00109) ditto
* [f40](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6730672100868096/html/sys-podman-fedora-40-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00109) rc=139, without an error message
* [f40 aarch64](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6220498705580032/html/sys-podman-fedora-40-aarch64-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00109) rc=139
* [rawhide](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5657548752158720/html/sys-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--00109) rc=139

sample subuid:
```
cevich:524288:65536
packer:589824:65536
lsm5:655360:65536
some21765dude:720896:65536
containers:200000:1048576
```
"Add a safe entry to sub[ug]id" _has_ to be a solved problem. Why can't I find the solution?

> "Add a safe entry to sub[ug]id" has to be a solved problem. Why can't I find the solution?

useradd should be safe I think but containers is not a real user just something we invented to "reverse" and define the range used by auto userns mode.


I mean AFAIK we have 4294967295 uids (u32) in the kernel so maybe just use a much larger base value that we do not use for our user ids. 
containers:1000000:1048576 maybe?
Cockpit tests failed for commit cf6a734dbf219dc04074aa980826cd127fd027df. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

It's `$TMPDIR`. If it is set, boom:
```console
# PODMAN=$(pwd)/bin/podman TMPDIR=/tmp bats --filter idmapped test/system/030-run.bats
...
[16:52:51.352402011] # /root/go/podman/bin/podman run --uidmap=0:1000:10000 --rm --rootfs /tmp/podman_bats.zhNCzh/rootfs:idmap=uids=0-1001-10000;gids=0-1002-10000 stat -c %u:%g /bin
[16:52:51.423426396] Error: failed to create idmapped mount: operation not permitted
[16:52:51.426333547] [ rc=126 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
```
(Cannot use `hack/bats` because it does not preserve TMPDIR).

As a bonus, it leaks a mount:
```console
# mount|grep rootfs
tmpfs on /tmp/podman_bats.zhNCzh/rootfs type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,nodev,idmapped,seclabel,size=1002808k,nr_inodes=1048576,inode64)
```
I have no idea where to take it from here. Hope this can help someone else.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 4831981cd98b83a54f8b71e795794b3eb36aaa9f. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

Well, `TMPFS= run_podman` fixes the debian and f39 crashes, but f40 & rawhide still break.

And, did something change recently with nftables? [Two crashes in one run](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5865078484893696/html/int-podman-fedora-40-root-container-sqlite.log.html):
```
           # podman [options] stop --all -t 0
           internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: Device or resource busy
         
           time="2024-07-24T22:18:04Z" level=error msg="Unable to clean up network for container d31fdf01596830d8d64f440c9d867db8e7d717fa7a98c391264c631e6745b36b: \"netavark: nftables error: nft did not return successfully while applying ruleset\""
           d31fdf01596830d8d64f440c9d867db8e7d717fa7a98c391264c631e6745b36b
           c1a63f6ee238021243da5ef27056000a42b12382f5f071fc386328ca7a77854f
```
thanks, that was very helpful.  I've added a couple of patches to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23395 that hopefully address the root cause
I've pushed a different version for my last patch, hopefully that helps with the e2e tests

> internal:0:0-0: Error: Could not process rule: Device or resource busy          
> time="2024-07-24T22:18:04Z" level=error msg="Unable to clean up network for container d31fdf01596830d8d64f440c9d867db8e7d717fa7a98c391264c631e6745b36b: \"netavark: nftables error: nft did not return successfully while applying ruleset\""

@edsantiago Please file a issue for that whatever that was it needs to be looked at, nftables testing is still very new if we find problems we should try to fix them as we are pushing out nftables as default for RHEL 10/Fedora. 
It it is not clear if the cause is nftables or some other race though.
@edsantiago are you fine if I take this over (and squash the fixup patch with yours)?

No, because you're on PTO and I'm a real jerk about leaving people be at peace when they should be at peace! Grrrrr!

But next week? Sure!

> No, because you're on PTO and I'm a real jerk about leaving people be at peace when they should be at peace! Grrrrr!
> 
> But next week? Sure!

no worries, it is a small thing.

Tests are green now!

Tests are green (Except for podman machine which always flakes these days) but this is not something I would commit: it includes my sloppy debug printfs, and a now-incorrect skip test. Shall I take it back over and clean it up?

> Tests are green (Except for podman machine which always flakes these days) but this is not something I would commit: it includes my sloppy debug printfs, and a now-incorrect skip test. Shall I take it back over and clean it up?

cleaned it up.  Does it look good to you?
tests are green
